description:
A sustainability specialist at a municipal utility in the Pacific Northwest needs to evaluate a proposed 500 kW run-of-river small hydropower installation on an existing irrigation canal. Create a Python script at /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/small_hydro_model.py that uses PySAM's Generic System and LCOE Fixed Charge Rate (Lcoefcr) models to simulate the project's performance and financials. 

The simulation should model a 500 kW system with a 0.95 derate factor and a heat rate of 0. The generation profile must be an 8760-hour array derived from the following monthly capacity factors (Jan-Dec): [0.45, 0.50, 0.65, 0.75, 0.80, 0.70, 0.50, 0.35, 0.30, 0.35, 0.45, 0.45], where each hour's output in kW is calculated as (Nameplate Capacity × Month's Capacity Factor × Derate Factor). The financial model must incorporate a total installed cost of $2,000,000, an annual fixed O&M cost of $50,000, a variable O&M cost of $0.005/kWh, and a fixed charge rate of 0.078. 

Save a JSON report to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/small_hydro_results.json containing the following keys: system_nameplate_kw, annual_energy_kwh, annual_capacity_factor_pct, monthly_energy_kwh (array of 12), lcoe_cents_per_kwh, and total_installed_cost_usd.
